records.ety: [OF. sevrer, severer, to separate, F. sevrer to wean, fr. L. separare. See Separate, and cf. Several.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Severed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Severing.]

defs: To separate, as one from another; to cut off from something; to divide; to part in any way, especially by violence, as by cutting, rending, etc.; as, to sever the head from the body.
quotes: The angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just.

defs: To cut or break open or apart; to divide into parts; to cut through; to disjoin; as, to sever the arm or leg.
quotes: Our state can not be severed; we are one.

defs: To keep distinct or apart; to except; to exempt.
quotes: I will sever in that day the land of Goshen, in which my people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there.

defs: To disunite; to disconnect; to terminate; as, to sever an estate in joint tenancy.
To suffer disjunction; to be parted, or rent asunder; to be separated; to part; to separate.
To make a separation or distinction; to distinguish.
quotes: The Lord shall sever between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt.
They claimed the right of severing in their challenge.
